description: Build a directed theorem dependency graph from Lean4/Mathlib: nodes are declarations, an edge A->B means A's proof uses B as a premise (extracted from Name anchors in tactic strings). Corpus from LeanDojo/Mathlib4 HuggingFace datasets (src/load_data.py).
motivation: A premise-dependency graph is the substrate for any unsupervised structural analysis of mathematics.
formal_results: 186K nodes, 309K edges; 61,542 theorems with tactic sequences; corpus 180,973 declarations and 259,580 tactic steps. Largest connected component ~92,594 nodes.
results_description: A large directed premise-dependency graph over Mathlib, the substrate for clustering and bridge analysis.
conclusions: The proof library yields one large dependency graph suitable for community detection.
